global_summary: The company has earned only 33% of the days the plan expected by today, and nearly half of the scheduled effort (49%) is showing up as ghost days — planned but never worked — leaving it 15.9 points behind with just 22 of 151 weekly targets closed. Not a single project gained a task since yesterday, and the recurring cause across the board is money and materials, not labor: Clifford Clack is stopped on a cashflow-driven brick delay, Lingyan and Kyle are stuck waiting on doors and fixtures, and several jobs slid a full week at once. Only Katie Holmes & Sean Springs is running ahead of plan, while Karim-Merchant sits right on the tolerance line and is still savable. The deepest holes are Clifford Clack, a full 79 points behind, and Ginger Brown, a closeout job stalled at 45% earned when the plan wanted it finished today. The fastest wins this week are the jobs with clear next moves: pour Karim's form board Tuesday, push Rohini's roof decking and Kyle's shutters to their Jul 13 dates, and get Martha's electrical rough-in inspected before it gets covered.

22NxUEJKTj6C.name: Ginger Brown
22NxUEJKTj6C.text: The plan expected this job essentially finished by today at 100%, but only 45% of the work is actually earned, leaving a 55-point hole — this is a closeout project stalled at the finish line with just 1 of 12 tasks done. Field notes show missed site photos on Jul 7 and 8 and an exterior door still not returned, though Milagros did log 19 photos on Jul 9, so someone is back on site. To save the week, PAINTING and the FINAL BUILDING INSPECTION both have to close today (Jul 10); the pre-walk-through (due Jul 9) and final cleaning (due Jul 8) are already overdue and need to be caught up immediately.

22PCPuWPndkt.name: Clifford Clack — Gym Addition
22PCPuWPndkt.text: Only 14% earned against a plan expecting 93% — the worst gap in the company at 79 points, with just 1 of 12 tasks done and no movement since yesterday. The field log is blunt about why: the job was stopped today because the bricks weren't bought, a delay coming straight from cashflow, so brick installation and trash haul both slipped to Jul 11 and the secret-door build to Jul 10. To stop the bleeding, free the cash for brick so the bricklayer can work Saturday as promised, and protect the glass install already booked for Monday.

22PK9BhEJtZn.name: Lingyan Qiu (CNG2025-458)
22PK9BhEJtZn.text: This job has earned 20.6% against 33.3% planned, a 12.7-point gap, with only 2 of 26 tasks complete. A full seven-day cascade hit the finish trades — tile install, prefab cabinets, plumbing trim and electrical trim all pushed a week — while the immediate target is the kitchen electrical trim install. The near-term save is buying the interior doors today as Romina flagged and clearing the door budget overrun (4 needed, only 2 quoted); Angel and Adriel are set to walk the job this weekend and may work Saturday and Sunday to recover.

22PMwhcSuqJD.name: Karim-Merchant
22PMwhcSuqJD.text: Earned 33.3% against 43.3% planned — a 10-point gap that sits right on today's tolerance line, so it is slipping but still catchable. Foundation cure, framing, exterior sheathing and door/window install each moved five days later, all downstream of the foundation not being formed yet. The week hinges on FOUNDATION: PLACE FORM BOARD; Efrain returns Sunday, so follow up to lock the form board for Tuesday and keep the five-day slip from growing into a bigger one.

22Nx8Uut2Qxs.name: Kyle Aubry - Project 01
22Nx8Uut2Qxs.text: Earned 20% against 40% planned, doubling the gap to 20 points with 3 of 22 tasks done and nothing new since yesterday. The finish trades slid on material timing — new carpet to Jul 15, backsplash tile to Jul 16, and this week's target WINDOW SHUTTERS install to Jul 17 — while kitchen pulls only partly arrived, with the rest due Jul 16. To save the week, get the shutter installation back on schedule and close the fireplace mantel by its Jul 13 date; meanwhile the 330 sq ft flooring change order at $1,145.71 needs a fast decision so it doesn't add yet another delay.

22PL9RSbYNs9.name: Rohini Williams (CNG2025-496)
22PL9RSbYNs9.text: Earned 17.9% against 28.6% planned, a 10.7-point gap with 2 of 18 tasks done. Framing is the choke point — exterior sheathing slipped to Jul 13, this week's target roof decking to Jul 13, and doors and windows to Jul 13, while shingles blew out 13 days to Jul 30. To hold the week, drive FRAMING: ROOF DECKING to its Jul 13 date; the framing material quote is already in the chat, so approve it quickly and remind the client about the client-provided pendant light before it stalls the electrical work.

22P2cmNJZcwJ.name: Stephanie and Marc Felton
22P2cmNJZcwJ.text: Earned 51% against 67.3% planned, a 16.3-point gap, but with a healthier 4 of 17 tasks done. The slips here are short, only two to three days, and driven by fixtures and material timing rather than labor — cabinet doors, electrical trim, glass and the gate all moved just a couple of days. The week's target is MECHANICAL: INSTALL MECHANICAL TRIM; since interior shower faucets land Jul 11 and the exterior set arrives Jul 15-16, lock the trim finishes now around the chosen brushed-gold spec so these small delays don't compound.

22PGVNmUgLww.name: Martha Cockrell (CNG-20251199)
22PGVNmUgLww.text: Earned 49.5% against 61.6% planned, a 12.1-point gap, but this job has the most tasks done in the company at 6 of 20, and the field log shows real momentum — mechanical and electrical rough-ins are complete, the whole MEP was walked, and an $18,300 electrical work order was issued. The alarming +31-day framing and +22-day electrical reschedules read like schedule cleanup rather than fresh slips, since two other rough-ins actually pulled earlier, to Jul 8 and Jul 10. The one thing to protect: get the electrical rough-in inspected before anyone covers it with insulation or drywall, then keep MECHANICAL ROUGH IN moving toward this week's target.

22P2j3sQMKqz.name: Katie Holmes & Sean Springs
22P2j3sQMKqz.text: The only project running ahead — 27.5% earned against 22.5% planned, a 5-point cushion in front of plan with 2 of 12 tasks done. The one reschedule was minor, drywall hang sheetrock slipping a single day to Jul 9, and that save-the-week deadline has effectively been met. Protect the momentum: the tape-and-bed, texture and paint work orders are already created with the sub starting Saturday, so lock the urgent tile, baseboard and trim, and door quotes now so the finish trades don't stall behind drywall.
